subject: English

lesson.story_mission: Join the Fact Forest crew to turn clear ideas into Grade 2 paragraphs.
lesson.objective: I can name a topic, supply facts, use linking words, and provide closure.
lesson.concept_explanation: Writers plan one clear idea, add connected details, and finish with a sentence that sounds complete.
lesson.common_misconception: Writers may give an answer without enough parts for the writing purpose.
lesson.real_world_connection: Clear writing helps classmates understand book choices, science facts, and real events.
lesson.lesson_summary: Plan the parts, write complete sentences, and reread with the checklist.
lesson.mini_lesson: Say the goal, choose strong details, then write sentences with capitals, spaces, and punctuation.

worked_examples.text: Watch: Plan the parts before you write the paragraph.
Demo: Reread the draft with the writing checklist.

hint_ladder: Use the visual model to plan your answer.
Check capitals, spaces, and punctuation before you finish.

level_banks.label: Level 3: Use Linking Words

questions.prompt: Add a linking word to connect this fact about butterflies: ___, butterflies begin as caterpillars.
questions.correct_answer: First
hints: Read the writing goal carefully.
Use the visual model to plan your answer.
Check capitals, spaces, and punctuation before you finish.
questions.explanation: The answer fits the writing target and stays on topic.
questions.feedback: Good writing move — it matches the checklist.

First

level_banks.label: Level 4: Build an Informative Paragraph
level_banks.focus: Write a topic sentence, facts, linking words, and closure.

questions.prompt: Write an informative paragraph about butterflies. Name the topic, add facts, use a linking word, and close.
questions.correct_answer: Butterflies are interesting to learn about. First, butterflies have wings. Also, butterflies begin as caterpillars. Now you know important facts about butterflies.
hints: Read the writing goal carefully.
Use the visual model to plan your answer.
Check capitals, spaces, and punctuation before you finish.
questions.explanation: The answer fits the writing target and stays on topic.
questions.feedback: Good writing move — it matches the checklist.
acceptable_answers: Butterflies are interesting to learn about. First, butterflies have wings. Also, butterflies begin as caterpillars. Now you know important facts about butterflies.
Butterflies is my topic. First, butterflies have wings. Also, butterflies begin as caterpillars. Now you know about butterflies.
questions.closing: Now you know important facts about butterflies.

questions.prompt: Write an informative paragraph about libraries. Name the topic, add facts, use a linking word, and close.

hints: Read the writing goal carefully.
Use the visual model to plan your answer.
Check capitals, spaces, and punctuation before you finish.
questions.explanation: The answer fits the writing target and stays on topic.
questions.feedback: Good writing move — it matches the checklist.

Libraries is my topic. First, libraries lend books. Also, a librarian helps readers. Now you know about libraries.
questions.closing: Now you know important facts about libraries.
